{{Class 2e}} '''Level π''' is a sub-level located in between [[Level 3]] and [[Level 4]]. [[File:Gas Station.png|right|250x250px|link=]] ==Description== '''Level π''' (pronounced "Level Pi") has an anomaly in which any sentences documenting/regarding the level compliance. The circumstances are undetermined; the author leaves the effect of this aberration unnoticed and is consistent with its supply. The word in which the level chooses to rhyme is the last word of the first sentence of the paragraph, meaning if one moves on to the next section, the anomaly chooses another word, then it repeats until the end document/article, standing by. Leastwise, the physical appearance of '''Level π''' is an expansive field, with the
satuЯation
of the grass revealed. Impossibly round hills resembling a hemisphere protruding out the ground haphazardly positioned throughout the level; noticeable grid concealed. In addition to wave-like patterns altering the elevation and depression of a region; asphalt roads curving and twisting into areas unsystematically without any justifications in the field. These are some characteristics within the level one could not possibly comprehend, as the logic is repealed. Furthermore, the level possesses an oversaturated appearance with highly exaggerated amalgamations of colors sealed. The effect stays consistent throughout the level, no matter where one is present throughout the level, even if it is the level's end repealed.

Multicolored gas stations seemingly placed in inconsistent locations are a standard for '''Level π''', surreal. However, most of these structures are commonly found beside asphalt roads — with only a certain amount found on the hills previously mentioned above and mountains that appeal. The interior of these gas stations does not appear to possess any signs of human life or intervention, with the cleanliness and the well-maintained appearance having no determined deals. Secondly, the logo/company these gas stations are supposedly owned by are distinguishable from [[the Frontrooms]], no indications of any company in '''Level π''' existing in [[the Frontrooms]]' ordeal. On certain occasions, a mist appearing to “lower down” the
satuЯation
of the level will congeal. As well as pixelating the region it covers, the effect may cause disorientation as it appears "unreal." The mist in question does not possess any apparent source from anywhere or anything, with cases reported by most witnesses indicating an instantaneous materialization under undetermined zeal. Most accounts of subjects bending down to direct contact and breathing in the mist had resulted in a fatality, while a lung collapse or a serious case of lung cancer was reported by others who had to heal. ==Colonies & Outposts== Most, if not all colonies ever established in '''Level π''' has been disbanded under unknown justifications. ==Entities== There are no documented entities in '''Level π'''. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== *Rarely, a frameless door in [[Level 60]]'s leads one to '''Level π''', with a window overlooking '''Level π''''s overview. *Quantum fluctuations in [[Level 450]] may teleport one here too. *Extensive cylindrical caves in [[The Hive]] will lead one to '''Level π''' if nobody is present in the level, similar to a queue. *Entering the first train based on the chronological order in [[The Metro]], or any locomotives labeled with the number two. ===Exits=== *Undescriptive circumstances/anomalies in '''Level π''' causes one to enter [[Level 94]].
